flashcards.definition: lim_{x→c} f(x) = L means f(x) → L as x → c.
lim_{x→c⁻} f(x); the limit as x approaches c from the left.
lim_{x→c⁺} f(x); the limit as x approaches c from the right.
The limit exists iff left-hand limit = right-hand limit.
lim_{x→c} f(x) can differ from f(c); the limit depends only on nearby values.
